source.title: Widespread access to predictive models in the motor system: a short review

confidence_rationale: Established review by Wolpert et al. on the pervasive access to forward models across oculomotor, skeletomotor, postural, perceptual, and mental-imagery systems. Direct grounding for the broader architectural principle that comparator-style predictive subtraction is not a localised mechanism but a substrate-wide computational primitive. Used here as background support for the SD-047 architecture rather than as load-bearing direct evidence: the review establishes that REE's commitment to a counterfactual_forward comparator (E2_harm_s) is consistent with biology, but it does not directly speak to the env-side substrate question.
failure_signatures: If REE's comparator architecture were idiosyncratic to one substrate level (e.g. only z_harm_s) while biology shows pervasive cross-system forward-model access, the architectural commitment may be too narrow -- but this is a critique of MECH-095 / ARC-033, not of SD-047 per se.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Recent behavioural and computational studies suggest that access to internal predictive models of arm and object dynamics is widespread in the sensorimotor system. Multiple systems -- oculomotor and skeletomotor control, perceptual processing, postural control, mental imagery -- are able to access predictions of the motion of the arm. A capacity to make and use predictions of object dynamics is similarly widespread.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-095 agency-detection comparator and MECH-098 reafference cancellation both require access to forward-model predictions of self-caused state change. Davidson and Wolpert establish that such access is pervasive in biological motor control, which validates the architectural commitment that REE's E2_harm_s and z_self forward circuits are doing biologically-realistic work. SD-047 is the env-side intervention that gives those forward models a non-trivial test: predict against a noisy multi-source background rather than a clean scheduled-hazard background.
mapping.mapping_caveat: This is a 2005 review of motor-system forward-model evidence; it is two architectural levels removed from the env-substrate question SD-047 raises. Used as background grounding rather than direct evidence. A cleaner direct citation for the cancellation architecture is Sawtell 2010 (cerebellum-like circuit); Davidson and Wolpert is the broader theoretical scaffold.
mapping.source_context: Review article in J Neural Engineering surveying behavioural and computational evidence across oculomotor, skeletomotor, postural, perceptual, and mental-imagery domains.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality solid for an established review. Mapping fidelity lower than the other entries because the paper is two steps removed from the env-substrate question. Used as background grounding rather than load-bearing evidence; net confidence ~0.70.
